questions.subject: NSEP
questions.q: If a system is made to undergo a change from an initial state to a final state by adiabatic process only, then
options: the work done is different for different paths connecting the two states.
there is no work done since there is no transfer of heat.
the internal energy of the system will change.
the work done is the same for all adiabatic paths.

questions.subject: NSEP
questions.q: A gas is made to undergo a change of state from an initial state to a final state along different paths by adiabatic process only. Therefore,
options: The work done is different for different paths.
The work done is the same for all paths.
There is no work done as there is no transfer of energy.
The total internal energy of the system will not change.

questions.subject: NSEP
options: the force of attraction between the molecules depends upon intermolecular distance.
internal energy depends only upon temperature.
internal energy is a function of both temperature and volume.
internal energy is a function of both temperature and pressure.

questions.subject: NSEP
questions.q: The molar specific heat capacity of a certain gas is expressed as $C=C_V+\alpha \frac{P}{T}$. The equation of state for the process can be written as ( $\alpha \& \mathrm{~A}$ are constant)

questions.subject: NSEP
questions.q: Consider the process of the melting of a spherical ball of ice originally at $0^{\circ}$ Assuming that the heat is being absorbed uniformly through the surface and the rate of absorption is proportional to the instantaneous surface area. Which of the following is true for the radius (r) of the ice ball at any instant of time? Assume that the initial radius of the ice ball at $t=0$ is $r=R_0$ and that the shape of the ball always remains spherical during melting. Also assume that L and $\rho$ are respectively the latent heat and density of ice at $0^{\circ}$
options: radius decreases exponentially with time as $r=R_0 e^{-\frac{k t}{\rho L}}$. Here k is constant
radius decreases exponentially with time as $r=R_0 e^{-\frac{k \rho t}{2 L}}$
radius of the ice ball decreases with time linearly with a slope $-\frac{k}{\rho L}$
radius of the ice ball decreases with time linearly with a slope $-\frac{k \rho}{2 L}$

questions.subject: NSEP
questions.q: The work done by the three moles of an ideal gas in the cyclic process $A B C D$ shown in the diagram is approximately. Given that $\mathrm{T}_1=100 \mathrm{~K}, \mathrm{~T}_2=200 \mathrm{~K}$ and $\mathrm{T}_3=600 \mathrm{~K}, \quad \mathrm{~T}_4=300 \mathrm{~K}$

questions.subject: NSEP
questions.q: An ideal monatomic gas is confined within a cylinder by a spring loaded piston of cross-sectional area $4 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{~m}^2$. Initially the gas is at 400 K and occupies a volume $2 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{~m}^3$ and the spring is in its relaxed position. The gas is heated by an electric heater for some time. During this time the gas expands and the piston moves out by a distance 0.1 m . The spring connected to the rigid wall is massless and frictionless. The force constant of the spring is $2000 \mathrm{Nm}^{-1}$ and atmospheric pressure is $10^5 \mathrm{Nm}^{-2}$ then
options: The final temperature of the gas is 720 K .
The work done by gas in expanding is 50 J
The heat supplied by heater is 190 J
The heat supplied by heater is 290 J

questions.subject: NSEP
questions.q: Two identical Carnot (cycles) engines operate between maximum and minimum temperatures $\mathrm{T}_1$ and $\mathrm{T}_2$ and volume limits, $\mathrm{V}_2, \mathrm{~V}_{\mathrm{b}}, \mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{c}} \& \mathrm{~V}_{\mathrm{d}}$ as shown in figure. Given that $\frac{\mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{c}}}{\mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{a}}}=\mathrm{e}^3$ and $\frac{\mathrm{T}_1}{\mathrm{~T}_2}=\mathrm{e}$ (e is the base of natural logarithm). Engine 1 operates on mono atomic gas while the engine 2 on diatomic gas. Choose correct alternatives
options: Ratio of work done per cycle for the two is $\frac{W_1}{W_2}=3$
Ratio of work per cycle for the two is $\frac{W_1}{W_2}=1$
Ratio of efficiencies ( $\eta$ ) of two engines $\frac{\eta_1}{\eta_2}=1$

questions.subject: NSEP

questions.subject: NSEP
questions.q: An ideal gas ( $n$ moles) is initially at pressure P and temperature T . It is cooled isochorically to a pressure $\frac{P}{4}$. The gas is then expanded at a constant pressure so as to attain back its initial temperature T. Work done by gas during the entire process is

questions.subject: NSEP
questions.q: Two moles of nitrogen in a container, of negligible thermal capacity, are initially at $17^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$. The gas is compressed adiabatically from an initial volume of 120 liter to 80 liter. The correct option(s) is/are
options: Initial pressure of the gas is nearly 40.2 kPa
Final temperature of the gas is nearly $68^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$
Work done by the gas is $2.12 k J$
The internal energy of the gas increases by $2.12 k J$
